tasks.title: Initialize Project Repository and Core Structure
tasks.description: Set up the project foundation with Node.js, npm configuration, directory structure, and essential configuration files
tasks.details: Create project directory structure including /scripts, /tasks, /.cursor/rules folders. Initialize npm project with package.json containing ES module configuration. Set up .gitignore for node_modules and .env files. Create .env.example with required environment variables (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, PERPLEXITY_API_KEY, MODEL, MAX_TOKENS, TEMPERATURE). Implement basic logging system with configurable levels. Setup Commander.js for CLI parsing.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify npm install completes successfully, all directories exist with proper permissions, .env.example contains all required variables, basic logging outputs to console with different levels, and Commander.js help command displays expected output

tasks.title: Implement Task Data Model and Validation
tasks.description: Create the core task data structure with JSON schema validation for tasks.json file format
tasks.details: Define Task and TaskCollection models matching the PRD specification. Implement Zod or similar validation library for type safety. Create TaskModel class with properties: id (number), title (string), description (string), status (enum: pending|done|deferred), dependencies (array of numbers), priority (enum: high|medium|low), details (string), testStrategy (string), subtasks (array). Implement validation methods for dependency chains (no circular dependencies, dependencies must exist). Create default tasks.json structure with meta object.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test task model creation with valid/invalid data, verify dependency validation catches circular references, test priority and status enum validation, ensure subtask structure validates correctly, verify tasks.json file can be read and written with proper schema validation

tasks.title: Build Core Task Management Operations
tasks.description: Implement CRUD operations for tasks including create, read, update, delete, and list functionality
tasks.details: Create TaskManager class with methods: loadTasks() to read tasks.json, saveTasks() to write tasks.json with atomic writes, getTask(id) to retrieve single task, getAllTasks() with filtering options, createTask(taskData) with ID generation, updateTask(id, updates) with validation, deleteTask(id) with dependency checking. Implement proper error handling for file operations. Add transaction-like behavior to prevent data corruption. Create backup before modifications.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all CRUD operations with sample data, verify atomic writes prevent corruption during failures, test filtering by status and priority, ensure dependency constraints are enforced on delete, verify backup creation before modifications, test concurrent access scenarios

tasks.title: Develop CLI Commands for Task Operations
tasks.description: Create command-line interface commands for listing, updating status, showing details, and managing tasks
tasks.details: Implement CLI commands using Commander.js: 'list' with filters (--status, --priority, --with-subtasks), 'show <id>' for detailed task view, 'update <id>' with --status/--priority options, 'done <id>' as shorthand for marking complete, 'defer <id>' for deferring tasks. Add colorized output using chalk or similar library. Implement table formatting for list view. Add --json flag for programmatic output. Handle invalid IDs gracefully with helpful error messages.
tasks.testStrategy: Test each command with valid and invalid inputs, verify filter combinations work correctly, test JSON output format matches specification, ensure error messages are helpful and specific, verify colorized output displays correctly, test edge cases like non-existent task IDs

tasks.title: Create Task File Generation System
tasks.description: Build system to generate individual task files from tasks.json data with proper formatting and bi-directional sync
tasks.details: Implement TaskFileGenerator class with methods: generateTaskFile(task) to create formatted .txt file, generateAllTaskFiles() for batch generation, parseTaskFile(filepath) to read task file format, syncTaskFiles() for bi-directional updates. Use template literals for consistent formatting. Include all task metadata in file header comments. Implement file naming as task_XXX.txt with zero-padded IDs. Handle subtasks in file format. Add file change detection for sync operations.
tasks.testStrategy: Test file generation produces correct format for all task fields, verify bi-directional sync maintains data integrity, test file parsing handles various formatting edge cases, ensure generated files are human-readable and editable, verify sync detects and merges changes correctly

tasks.title: Integrate Anthropic Claude API
tasks.description: Implement Claude API client with authentication, error handling, and response parsing for AI operations
tasks.details: Create ClaudeClient class using Anthropic SDK. Implement authentication using ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from environment. Add methods: sendPrompt(prompt, options) with streaming support, parseResponse(response) for structured data extraction. Implement exponential backoff for rate limiting. Add retry logic for transient failures. Create prompt template system for consistent formatting. Handle API errors gracefully with fallback behavior. Implement token counting and cost tracking.
tasks.testStrategy: Mock API responses for unit tests, test authentication with invalid keys, verify exponential backoff timing, test response parsing with various formats, ensure error handling doesn't crash application, verify token counting accuracy, test streaming response handling

tasks.title: Build PRD Parsing and Task Generation
tasks.description: Create system to parse PRD files and generate initial task list using Claude AI with intelligent analysis
tasks.details: Implement PRDParser class with methods: readPRD(filepath) for file loading, generateTasks(prdContent) using Claude API, inferDependencies(tasks) for smart dependency detection, assignPriorities(tasks) based on dependency order. Create specialized prompts for PRD analysis. Implement response validation to ensure proper task format. Add logic to detect logical task ordering. Create mapping between PRD sections and tasks. Handle various PRD formats (markdown, plain text).
tasks.testStrategy: Test with sample PRDs of varying complexity, verify generated tasks have valid structure, test dependency inference logic with known patterns, ensure priorities align with dependencies, test error handling for malformed PRDs, verify task count matches PRD complexity

tasks.title: Implement Task Expansion with Subtasks
tasks.description: Build functionality to expand tasks into detailed subtasks using Claude AI with context awareness
tasks.details: Create TaskExpander class with methods: expandTask(taskId, numSubtasks) for single task, expandAllTasks(options) for batch processing, generateSubtaskPrompt(task, context) for prompt creation. Implement parent-child relationship management in data model. Add subtask ID generation (parent.child format). Create context gathering from related tasks. Implement acceptance criteria generation for subtasks. Add progress tracking for batch operations.
tasks.testStrategy: Test subtask generation produces actionable items, verify parent-child relationships are maintained, test ID generation follows specification, ensure subtasks align with parent task goals, test batch processing with progress updates, verify acceptance criteria are specific and measurable

tasks.title: Add Perplexity API Integration
tasks.description: Integrate Perplexity API for research-backed task enhancement with fallback to Claude
tasks.details: Create PerplexityClient class using OpenAI SDK compatibility. Implement authentication with PERPLEXITY_API_KEY. Add methods: research(query) for online search, enhanceTask(task) for research-backed details. Implement fallback mechanism to Claude when unavailable. Create specialized research prompts for technical topics. Add response quality comparison between Perplexity and Claude. Cache research results to minimize API calls. Implement cost tracking for both APIs.
tasks.testStrategy: Test API integration with mock responses, verify fallback to Claude works seamlessly, test research quality for technical topics, ensure caching reduces duplicate calls, verify cost tracking accuracy, test error handling for API failures

tasks.title: Create Cursor AI Integration Documentation
tasks.description: Develop comprehensive Cursor rules and workflow documentation for AI agent integration
tasks.details: Create .cursor/rules directory structure. Write dev_workflow.mdc with task discovery and selection guidelines. Develop cursor_rules.mdc with command syntax and agent patterns. Create self_improve.mdc for iterative enhancement. Document agent-user interaction patterns. Include example workflows for common scenarios. Create command cheatsheet for agents. Document context management strategies. Add troubleshooting section for common agent issues.
tasks.testStrategy: Review documentation with sample agent interactions, test command examples work as documented, verify workflow guidelines are clear and actionable, ensure documentation covers all CLI commands, test that agents can follow the documented patterns, validate examples are accurate and helpful

tasks.title: Implement Batch Operations and Advanced Features
tasks.description: Add batch task operations, filtering, and project initialization capabilities
tasks.details: Implement batch operations: updateMultiple(ids, updates), markAllDone(filter), deferMultiple(ids). Add advanced filtering with query language support. Create project initialization wizard with interactive prompts. Implement task templates for common patterns. Add task import/export functionality (JSON, CSV). Create task dependency visualization. Implement task search with fuzzy matching. Add configuration profiles for different project types.
tasks.testStrategy: Test batch operations maintain data integrity, verify filters work with complex queries, test initialization wizard creates valid project structure, ensure import/export maintains all task data, verify search finds relevant results, test configuration profiles apply correctly

tasks.title: Build Implementation Drift Handling
tasks.description: Create system to update future tasks based on implementation changes and new context
tasks.details: Implement DriftHandler class with methods: detectDrift(completedTasks, futureTasks) to identify outdated tasks, updateFutureTasks(context) using AI analysis, preserveCompleted() to protect finished work, recalculateDependencies() for chain updates. Create prompts for context-aware task updates. Implement change tracking to show what was modified. Add user confirmation for significant changes. Create rollback mechanism for unwanted updates.
tasks.testStrategy: Test drift detection identifies outdated tasks correctly, verify updates preserve completed work, test dependency recalculation maintains validity, ensure user can review and approve changes, verify rollback restores previous state, test with various implementation change scenarios
